title: dianthus attenuatus subsp. requienii
intro: Dianthus attenuatus subsp. requienii, commonly known as Corsican pink or Requien's pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a low-growing, spreading perennial with delicate pink flowers and is native to the island of Corsica. Dianthus attenuatus subsp. requienii has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. This plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be beneficial for overall health and wellness, particularly in supporting immune function and promoting relaxation.
